books.title: Building AI Agents
books.summary: From static LLMs to production-ready agents — state, tools, memory, and the boundaries that keep them safe to ship.

books.title: 100 Things Every Data Engineer Should Know
books.summary: One hundred practitioner-tested lessons on building reliable data pipelines — storage, orchestration, quality, streaming, governance, and the mindset shifts that make data teams effective.

books.title: 100 Things Every Programmer Should Know
books.summary: One hundred hard-won lessons from experienced engineers — on code quality, design, testing, security, collaboration, and the professional habits that separate good engineers from great ones.

books.title: AI-Assisted Code Refactoring
books.summary: How LLMs and agentic tools change the economics of refactoring at codebase scale — the prompt patterns, validation pipelines, review strategies, and risk management that make mass-refactor campaigns reliable.

books.title: AWS Essentials
books.summary: The core services and patterns that power production on AWS — compute, storage, networking, databases, security, and the architectural thinking that holds it all together.

books.title: Azure Essentials
books.summary: The core services and patterns behind production on Azure — compute, containers, storage, networking, identity, and the architectural thinking that keeps cloud costs and complexity in check.

books.title: CI/CD Pipelines: Design, Deliver, Govern
books.summary: CI/CD as a design discipline — pipeline topology, branching strategies, security gates, supply-chain attestations (SLSA, Sigstore, SBOM), DORA metrics, and the patterns that ship software safely at scale.

books.title: Communicating with Data
books.summary: How to turn a chart into a message — stripping clutter, using color and annotation as tools, and building the habits that make data communication consistently clear.

books.title: Communication Patterns for Engineers
books.summary: The patterns that make technical communication work — from ADRs and RFCs to stakeholder updates, incident comms, and pull request descriptions that actually get merged.

books.title: Critical Thinking for Engineers
books.summary: Structured reasoning for technical decisions — recognizing cognitive biases, evaluating vendor claims and benchmarks, and staying analytical under the pressure of incidents and deadlines.

books.title: Designing APIs
books.summary: API design as architecture — REST, GraphQL, gRPC, event-driven, versioning, security, gateways, observability, and the AI-era shapes (MCP, tool-calling, agent-native APIs) that are reshaping what an API contract means.

books.title: Effective Data Visualization
books.summary: The principles behind charts that communicate clearly — from aesthetics mapping and color theory to modern web tooling, accessibility, and the charts people get wrong most often.

books.title: Effective Software Testing
books.summary: Test design as engineering — specification-based and structural techniques, test doubles, property-based testing, testability as a design property, TDD as practiced not preached, and the AI-era shifts that automate the rote without replacing the judgment.

books.title: Emotional Intelligence at Work
books.summary: Why EI is load-bearing in engineering — self-awareness, empathy, feedback, difficult conversations, and the skills that keep cross-team work from collapsing under pressure.

books.title: Engineering Data Systems
books.summary: The lifecycle that turns raw source data into something useful — generation, ingestion, storage, transformation, serving — and the undercurrents (security, governance, orchestration) that hold every stage together.

books.title: LLM Patterns
books.summary: Design patterns for building real applications on top of language models — grounding, retrieval, reliability, action, and the rest of the production toolkit.

books.title: Platform Engineering Foundations
books.summary: Internal developer platforms as a product — paved roads, golden paths, self-service provisioning, developer experience metrics, and the org patterns (Team Topologies) that decide whether a platform team multiplies or bottlenecks every other team.

books.title: Software Architecture
books.summary: Thinking, modularity, characteristics, and the dozen styles every architect picks between — a working playbook for system design.

books.title: Testing AI and ML Systems
books.summary: How to test AI and ML systems — quality characteristics, data quality, fairness, adversarial robustness, and the LLM-era extensions of the ISTQB® CT-AI syllabus. About AI as the system under test. Helps prepare for the ISTQB® CT-AI exam; not an official ISTQB® study guide and not affiliated with or endorsed by ISTQB®. ISTQB® is a registered trademark of the International Software Testing Qualifications Board.

books.title: The Data Engineering Pattern Catalog
books.summary: A catalog of recurring patterns for data systems — from ingestion to serving — with the tradeoffs, failure modes, and 2026-era updates every practicing data engineer needs to know.

books.title: The Refactoring Catalog
books.summary: A working catalog of the refactorings that turn maintainable code into great code — code smells that trigger them, mechanical recipes for applying them safely, and the AI-era tooling that's reshaping how we do it at scale.

books.title: Working with DuckDB
books.summary: A practical guide to DuckDB — the embedded analytical SQL database that runs everywhere, queries anything, and makes local-first data work fast and simple.
